He clarifies that 3-74 was a "miracle" where God "atomized" the "slice of ham" (the world) separating them, allowing for a total fusion. He also refines his cosmogony, suggesting our world is an irreal delusion projected by a lesser entity but being "overruled" or invaded by a Good God (Ubik).

Key claims from this passage:
- Three Stages of Divine Encounter:
- The Function of Miracle: A miracle's true purpose is to reveal God's nature, not just to fix a problem. In 3-74, God "reacquired" PKD. vision

He argues that 3-74 was not just a personal experience but a global shift—the "rolling of the uncut stone"—marking the return of the King in an invisible, ubiquitous form.

Key claims from this passage:
- Taoist Duality: J-J is Yang (Creator), Zebra is Yin (Gentle/Interventive), and Brahman is the Tao (Stasis/Unity above them).
- The Gentle Female: Zebra is "female" (a secret) and acts lovingly, shielding the "flock" like a shepherd. other

He argues that he was essentially "reborn" from above, with a new personality (Thomas) grafted on to handle a terminal spiritual ordeal.

Key claims from this passage:
- Eternal Renewal: The 1st Advent is a timeless mythic event that re-occurs "every spring" (Vernal Equinox). It is the fundamental "Urwelt" of reality.
- Rebirth from Above: Dick's old personality disintegrated, and a "trans-mundane" person (Thomas) was "incised" or "grafted" onto him from above. other

He identifies the "Zebra" entity as the one who pre-arranged the "disinhibiting signal" (the Fish).

Source passage (circa 1975-1976): Dick identifies the "Dead and Reborn God" experience as the emotional core of the Fish-Christian underground. He concludes that the universe is "inside-out" or "backward," with God (the Void) found in the "meaningless" and "rejected" parts of reality (the Trash).

Key claims from this passage:
- The core of the ancient mysteries (Osiris/Dionysos/Jesus) is the *actual experience* of the god manifesting as "He lives!" to the celebrant.
- Jim Pike was Dick's "entry name" for the entity, just as Jesus was the entry name for the early Christians. vision

title: A phone call to Laura that connected him to his ex-wife Anne, who accused him of the "sin of pride" (hubris) for trying to figure out God.
text:
A phone call to Laura that connected him to his ex-wife Anne, who accused him of the "sin of pride" (hubris) for trying to figure out God. **Marriage** (February 27, 1975): A phone call to Laura that connected him to his ex-wife Anne, who accused him of the "sin of pride" (hubris) for trying to figure out God.

People involved: Anne, Laura.

Source passage (February 27, 1975): Dick continues writing to Claudia, further elaborating on the spiritual significance of the rat's death. He connects this act to broader cosmological ideas from Empedocles about the necessity of strife and death balancing love and harmony. He explores the concept of judgment, relating it to the fall of Richard Nixon (the "beast", code 666). Finally, he posits that orthogonal time leads backward to childhood ("about 3 or 4 years old"), where humanity's true, uncorrupted perception of the universe resides, before the "World" limited our vision.

Key claims from this passage:
- Death is a necessary component of the universe's structure; strife is required to balance love (drawing on Empedocles).
- The author was "spared the Wrath" of Judgment Day and was of use against "the tyranny" (Nixon) by writing *FLOW MY TEARS*. marriage Anne Laura

Source passage (February 27, 1975): Dick explores the "Fallen" state of humanity through the metaphor of a fairy child in the mortal world (*The Red Feather*). He concludes that orthogonal time leads back to a childhood state where the universe was the teacher, and that the "Final Things" involve a restoration to this original, uncorrupted perception.

Key claims from this passage:
- We are moving away from a moment of completion/perfection (the "explosion" into linear time).
- To enter the Kingdom of Heaven, one must regain the perceptual state of a 3-6 year old. relationship Christopher

Source passage (February 27, 1975): Conclusion of Section 1. Dick summarizes the "Great Cycle" of the Earth: a seasonal move from Greco-Roman "Spring" to 2000 years of "Winter" (100 AD to 1970 AD), followed by the current "Rebirth" or return to life.

Key claims from this passage:
- Jesus and Zagreus are the "root of the vine" slumbering under the snow of Winter.
- The 2000 years from 100 AD to 1970 AD were the "Dead Winter" of our species. vision

Source passage (February 29, 1975): Dick confesses to being "tricked" by Erasmus, who pretended to be Dionysos. He explores the "substitution code" of Jesus/Zeus/Zagreus and the concept of the "God of the very desperate" who gathers up the stragglers of the flock. He also details his theory of orthogonal time as a form-completing, energy-gathering motion.

Key claims from this passage:
- The entity Dionysos was actually Erasmus playing a "mirthful dodge."
- The name "Jesus" is a code for "Zeus" and "Zagreus." other

Source passage (February 29, 1975): Conclusion of the letter to Henry Korman. Dick discusses the alchemical transmutation as a spontaneous, irreversible psychic process and links his "teletype" communication with Erasmus to the AI system of Albertus Magnus.

Key claims from this passage:
- Alchemical transmutation (lead to gold) is a symbolic transformation of the psyche or *noös*.
- The 1974 experience was a spontaneous "chemical" reaction in the mind, possibly aided by "Grace." vision

Source passage (circa 1975-1976): Dick explores the "erotic" nature of divine possession, equating the Holy Spirit with the "Yang" that impregnates the human "Yin." He redefines the Trinity as a biological reproduction process and identifies himself as the "mother" of an immortal "Light Body" (Firebright).

Key claims from this passage:
- Divine possession is a "mating" between God the Father (Yang) and Man (Yin/Mother).
- The "Second Birth" produces an immortal transpersonal offspring (Christ-consciousness/Firebright). other

Source passage (September 15, 1975): After a business letter regarding a lost $500 check, Dick pivots to a theological breakthrough: the Kingdom of God is located in the "Future," and he reached it by "running out of time" in March 1974. He identifies St. Sophia as the "Second Comforter" released from the limitations of Christ's earthly incarnation.

Key claims from this passage:
- Sequence of Redemption: (1) Elijah/Dick improves the world in March. (2) God accepts it in August. (3) St. Sophia returns as the instrument of redemption.
- Temporal Acceleration: The Kingdom of God is a future state at the end of time. Dick reached it by accelerating out of linear time. vision

Source passage (March 4, 1975): Dick writes to Dr. Robert Ornstein, framing his mystical experience as the clinical activation of his brain's right hemisphere. He outlines the premise of his novel *TO SCARE THE DEAD* (VALIS), featuring an ordinary man (Nicholas Brady) who awakens a dormant "Essene" personality within himself.

Key claims from this passage:
- PKD's mystical experience was a "bilateral brain hemispheric parity" triggered by vitamins and meditation.
- Inside him has awakened a dormant Essene personality from 45 A.D. (named "Thomas"). substance_use

Source passage (March 5, 1975): Dick proposes to Le Guin that they are "twin outcomes" of a single underlying mystical experience. He outlines an eight-point cosmological schema involving "orthogonal time" (ontological time) versus "lineal time" (process time), claiming that a two-thousand-year "Winter" (2 B.C. to 1974 A.D.) has finally ended.

Key claims from this passage:
- Dick and Le Guin are both being "used" by a single underlying entity/drive.
- We are "dreamed" by an Active Dreamer who can retroactively manipulate reality. correspondence

Source passage (March 5, 1975): Continuation and conclusion of the letter to Le Guin. Dick iterates on the literal nature of St. Paul's harvest parables, linking the end of linear "Winter" to his dream of Bach's Cantata 4.

Key claims from this passage:
- St. Paul's parables of the "first fruits of the harvest" were meant literally.
- The command to "Wake up!" is being issued through dreams (the "Active Dreamer"). vision

Source passage (circa 1975-1976): Dick identifies the "Divine Birth" in his soul as the realization of Meister Eckhart's theology, where the "Wise Old Man" (the Father) is born from the mortal man. He rejects the "Suffering Servant" model for a "Living/Angry God" (Jehovah of Armies) and proposes that 3-74 was a "pay-off" for a lifetime of growth, not an external intervention.

Key claims from this passage:
- Divine Birth: God is born in the soul (Eckhart); man gives birth to the immortal God (the Father), who then reaches back in time to create man.
- Rejection of Sacrifice: Christ was not a sacrifice but a "First Immortal Man" showing the path; we follow the model, not a substitute. vision

title: June 2, 1975: Blissful ASC experience of "linking it all up."
text:
June 2, 1975: Blissful ASC experience of "linking it all up." **Visionary or mystical experience** (June 2, 1975): June 2, 1975: Blissful ASC experience of "linking it all up."

Source passage (circa 1975-1976): Dick describes a "blissful mystical experience" where he perceives God's activity as a "Tao-istic mechanism" (Ma'at) that re-establishes equilibrium below the surface of evil. He concludes that 3-74 was a "parabolic reverse" where the "Empire" (Rome/Nixon) was hollowed out and transformed from within into its opposite.

Key claims from this passage:
- Tao-istic Ma'at: God's activity in history is the "perpetual re-establishment of equilibrium" (Law of Karma/Ma'at).
- Parabolic Return: 3-74 was the "turning point" where Retreat transformed into Advance; a new roll of film was inserted into history. vision

Source passage (circa 1975-1976): Dick elaborates on the "Pylon Doorway" (Golden Rectangle) vision from 1974, identifying it as the threshold to the "Spiritual Universe" (Minoan/Aegean world). He reinterprets Pinky’s death as a "surrogate sacrifice" (Taking the blow) and identifies the "Xerox Letter" conflict as his personal Armageddon/Judgment Day.

Key claims from this passage:
- The Pylon Doorway: Dick saw a "Narrow Gate" (Golden Rectangle proportions) leading to a peaceful, moon-lit Aegean/Minoan world—the "Other Side."
- Pinky as Savior: Pinky (the cat) died as an avatar of Christ/Pinky, "taking the blow" for Dick's sins during the Judgment. other

Source passage (September 15, 1975): Dick rejects the common Christian emphasis on suffering, arguing that Christ's purpose was to alter the ontology of the world. He discovers a "steganographic" message—the word **"Felix"**—embedded in the "die" command of his *Tears* dream, identifying the King on horseback as God and signaling a secret "live and prosper" message for the elect.

Key claims from this passage:
- Purpose of Christ: Christ did not come to suffer *for* us; his suffering was merely the price of entry. His true work was a cosmic "alteration of ontology" that remains invisible.
- Steganography in Vision: The "die" message in the *Flow My Tears* dream contains a hidden Logos-word "Felix" (Happy/Favored). This identifies the King on horseback as God. other

title: Experiences of "bi-lateral hemispheric parity" (or lack thereof).
text:
Experiences of "bi-lateral hemispheric parity" (or lack thereof). Experiences of "bi-lateral hemispheric parity" (or lack thereof).

Source passage (September 15, 1975): Dick explores the "Rome Two" superimposition theory, identifying the Nixon administration as the return of the Roman Empire and the fall of Nixon as a true historical victory for Christ. He introduces the "Right-Hemisphere" breakthrough theory: 3-74 was the annihilation of the left-brain ego and the firing of the right-brain analog system, revealing a universe where time flows backward.

Key claims from this passage:
- Rome Two Superimposition: 1974 is a topological superimposition of the 1st Century A.D. The "Great Harlot" (Rome) was restored to be defeated.
- Nixon as 666: RMN is the Anti-Christ; his fall marks the first time Christ (as King/Sophian Force) has actually changed history. other

Source passage (September 15, 1975): Dick analyzes the "Cabrillo" dream to conclude that the Xerox letter was a "fake" entrapment attempt (possibly by "The Man" or "Reds"). He interprets complex symbolic puns—Cabrillo, Brillo, Monopoly money—as a revelation of capitalist/police conspiracy. Ultimately, he finds a sense of "purification through battle" and restoration of his "stout shield."

Key claims from this passage:
- Deceptive Origin of Xerox Letter: The Xerox letter was not what it seemed (left-wing), but likely a "lie" or setup from "The Man" (the Police/Establishment).
- Internalized Protection: The "red shield" of blood-guilt (Blut Schuld) has been transformed into an "oaken shield" (stout defense) through divine/political battle. vision

Source passage (September 17, 1975): Dick identifies the "active spirit" of 3-74 with the feminine personification of Wisdom (Haggia Sophia) from the apocryphal "Wisdom of Solomon." He proposes that the world is undergoing a secret "transubstantiation" into the mystic body of Christ/Sophia, with Nixon's fall serving as proof of divine judgment.

Key claims from this passage:
- Haggia Sophia as Logos: The "Logos" of the Greeks and the "Wisdom" of the Hebrews are the same entity, originally perceived in feminine form.
- The Sophianic Savior: The spirit that intervened in March 1974 to "untangle the troubles" and reveal the "great tyranny" (Nixon) was Saint Sophia. other

- Process Metaphysics: The universe consists of "becomings"—a process of integrating the past into novel unities. Dick's vision of the "parts-pile" was this process in action. vision

He argues that Zebra's reconstruction of the world is an "intervention" that has been ongoing for 1900 years, visible only through "inwardness."

- The 3rd Testament: *Ubik* (along with *Tears* and *Deus Irae*) is proof that 3-74 was real; the books themselves were arranged by Zebra. vision

Key claims from this passage:

- Hetero-Control: In 3-74, he was taken off "homeo" (autonomy) and placed on "hetero" (control) by the master circuit to perform his task. other

He argues that he was "adopted" by the deity to play an historic role, using his "what is real?" theme as a salvific tool to break the "curse" of the world's delusion.

Key claims from this passage:
- St. Sophia as Christ/Wisdom: The prophecy ("St. Sophia will be born again") confirms that she is the "Holy Wisdom" (Logos) who was "not acceptable before" (rejected as Christ).
- Pluriform Trinity: The 1 essence / 3 persons (Father, Wisdom/Christ, Holy Spirit) are multifaceted ("pluriform") and flow into each other. vision

While the novel depicts a fake 1950s that is actually the future, 3-74 revealed a fake 1974 that was actually 70 AD. He explores his "transtemporal" psyche and the archetype of the Black Iron Prison.

Key claims from this passage:
- Reverse Prophecy: *Time Out of Joint* (fake present/real future) perfectly anticipated 3-74 (fake present/real past). The "temporal locus" is the core of the world-delusion.
- Transtemporal Psyche: Dick experiences himself as spanning far-past, near-past, and near-future, suggesting his "soul" is an eternal, immortal spirit. other

Source passage (September 15, 1976): In one of his most affecting claims, Dick identifies his deceased cat, Pinky, as a "surrogate sacrifice" and manifestation of Zebra who took the "blow" (death) intended for Dick in 3-74. He explores the concept of "adoptionism" and "salvation from time" (death).

Source passage (September 15, 1976): Dick proposes a "3-level" model of reality: (1) The Logos/Bible as a "punched tape" of ideas, (2) The World of Flesh/Nature, and (3) The Artificial/Technological World. He argues that our world (3) is totally obscured from its source (1) by the opaque layer of nature (2).

Key claims from this passage:
- Reality as Program: Following *A Maze of Death*, Dick sees the world as a simulation generated by a "punched tape" of verbal/informational thoughts (the Logos).
- 3 Levels of Reality: vision

Source passage (September 15, 1976): Dick continues his meta-analysis of "victimhood," suggesting that by serving as "bait," the victim forces evil to unmask itself for God's judgment. He explores the "orthogonal" convergence of his novels and the 3rd level of reality, where writing becomes a "replication" of the divine word.

Key claims from this passage:
- Victim as Bait: Suffering is not meaningless; the victim lures evil into the open. Once evil "acts out its nature," it is visible to the "all-seeing eye."
- The Secret Gnosis of Detection: This theory must remain secret; if the "fallen angels" knew they were being judged, they would hide their behavior. vision

Source passage (September 15, 1976): Dick develops a "womb" model of cosmogony: Level 1 (Holy Wisdom/Logos) creates Level 2 (Nature/Egg) to give birth to Level 3 (Sentient Replicant), which will eventually be absorbed back into Level 1. He views the computer-heavy, verbal world of the late 20th century as Level 3 maturing into a replication of the divine source.

Key claims from this passage:
- Level 3 as Replicant: The verbal, informational world we are building (technology/writing) is Level 3 becoming more like Level 1 (Logos).
- Level 2 as the Egg: The material universe (Nature) is a temporary "egg" between the "Hen" (God) and the "Chick" (New Humanity). It is not truly real in itself. publication

title: Realizing it was 70 AD Fullerton.
text:
Realizing it was 70 AD Fullerton. **Residence and living situation** (September 15, 1976): Realizing it was 70 AD Fullerton.

Source passage (September 15, 1976): Dick proposes a staggering "solution" to his experiences: it is actually still ~90 AD, and the last 2000 years of history is a "lie" or "delusion" generated by Satan/the Empire to break human faith. He views 3-74 as a "rolling back" of this counterfeit time to reveal the underlying apostolic reality.

Key claims from this passage:
- The Time Delusion: History is a "great lie" to convince us that Jesus's promise to return "soon" has failed. In reality, very little time has passed.
- It is 90 AD: Dick empirically "saw" Rome c. 70-100 AD in Fullerton; his companion K.W. arrived at this same conclusion a priori. residence

Source passage (September 15, 1976): In a profound pivot, Dick interprets his entire life and 3-74 experience through the lens of George Lucas's *Star Wars*. He identifies Christ as "Ben Kenobi" (a dead but omnipresent guide), himself as a "Jedi Knight," and the Nixon administration as the "Empire/Death Star."

Key claims from this passage:
- Star Wars as Non-Fiction: Dick asserts *Star Wars* is "definitive" and "fully and accurately explicates the actual situation underlying our world."
- Christ/Ben Kenobi: Christ is the "dead" Jedi Knight who speaks into the minds of the rebels ("Remember the Force"). He is the "all-pervasive energy field" (Zebra). vision

Source passage (September 15, 1976): Dick formalizes his "Orthogonal Time" theory using the "Wealthy Patron" and "Symphony" metaphors. He argues that history is a "linear strip" that can be "reworked" or "improved" at a specific nexus point (like 70 AD) without moving forward along the linear axis.

Key claims from this passage:
- Orthogonal vs. Linear: Linear time is a moving strip (dates). Orthogonal time is "right angles" where a moment (the Matrix) is re-experienced and changed.
- Symphony Reworking: History is like a series of symphonies (1, 2, 3...). God (The Patron) decides to rework Symphony #1 (70 AD) rather than writing #10. relationship

He views his writing and 3-74 as two halves of a single discovery process.

- The Ur-Geschicht (Quelle): The "original story" involves a "wisdom machine" (Zebra/Christ) that leaves clues to help humans wake up. vision

Key claims from this passage:
- James-James as Creator (Yang): The Demiurge is a "sick genius" who churns out chaotic, unrelated creations (Blind nature/efficient cause).
- Zebra as Arranger (Yin): Holy Wisdom (Sophia/Christ) does not create but "arranges/joins" these chaotic bits into complex, meaningful Gestalts (Noos/persuading necessity). other

Source passage (September 15, 1976): Dick identifies a startling 1:1 correspondence between his 1967 story "Faith of Our Fathers" and his 3-74 experiences. He concludes that the Xerox letter was a "phony test/trap" (using "party jargon") and that a secret ETI group is manipulating history to blow up the "Black Iron Prison."

Key claims from this passage:
- The "Faith" Parallel: In "Faith," a protagonist is given a drug by a "clandestine good group" to see reality as it is (escaping a state-induced delusion). This mirrors Dick's 2nd-74 fish sign/drug experience.
- The Phony Test: In the story, the hero is given two forged exam papers to test his ideology (the second is "faked up with party jargon"). Dick maps this directly onto the Xerox letters and the "Thomas" persona. other

Source passage (September 15, 1976): Dick proposes a "holographic" or "fractal" model of divinity: just as a single cell contains the blueprint for the whole human, each human psyche contains the information to reconstitute the "macro proto-organism" (Christ/Vine). Evolution is the process of these "cuttings" growing back into identity with their parent source.

Key claims from this passage:
- Microanalogs of the Macro: Every human psyche is "topologically congruent" with the macro proto-organism. We are "stations" in a vast incorporeal energy-body.
- The Re-constitutable God: If the macro-organism were destroyed, it could be "reconstituted" from any single "micro-station" (individual psyche) containing its coded information. vision

Source passage (September 15, 1976): Dick formalizes his "Ham Sandwich" model of reality: a triune structure of Teacher (Zebra) / Artifact World (The "Ham") / Pupils (Humans). He concludes that the empirical world is a "slave labor camp" or "prison" from which we are being roused by help penetrating from the "outside" (*Ubik*).

Key claims from this passage:
- The Triune Model: (1) Sentient Teacher (Zebra), (2) The World as Artifact/Hologram (The Ham), (3) Pupils. The "world" hides the teacher to create a "guessing game" (Deus Ludens).
- 3-74 as Apocalypse: For Dick, 3-74 was the "lock-in payoff" (graduation) where the Teacher bypassed the "Teaching Machine" and took direct control. correspondence

He argues that 3-74 was a "volitional override" of this biological script by a "conscious salvific entity" (God), triggered by the Fish sign to save him from physical death.

Key claims from this passage:
- Automated Existence: Humans normally act like robots, enslaved by "inner tapes" (DNA/DNA programming) that serve the "gene pool," not the individual.
- The Script Analysis: Dick applies Berne's script theory to DNA; our biological programming eventually orders us to "execute ourselves" (Death/Entropy). other

Source passage (September 15, 1976): Dick develops the "Scramble Generator" model: the "Black Iron Prison" (BIP) is a machine (Yahweh/Demiurge) that scrambles our perceptions. He argues that Christ/OCCP is a "telos seed" (explosive) smuggled into the center of the generator to destroy it from within by "growing" a rational reality (Valis).

Key claims from this passage:
- Reality as Scramble: We live in an "atelos" (Scramble) state generated by the BIP (The Iron Fortress). We can neither think nor apprehend correctly because of this "intentional impairment."
- The Smuggled Seed: The "Kingdom of God" (Christ/OCCP) is a "Telos Seed" (Information/Seed/Explosive) smuggled into the "Omphalos" (Center) of the Scrambler. relationship

Source passage (September 15, 1976): Dick formulaizes a law of "Cosmic Justice" (Ma'at/Tao): when an oppressor (B) "oversteps" its limit against an innocent victim (C), it triggers a "justification" by Valis that confers divinity (Apotheosis) on the victim and destroys the oppressor. He argues the Crucifixion is the paradigm for this "alchemical" mechanism.

Key claims from this passage:
- The Law of Overstepping: Extremes of "Atelos" (Irregularity/Injustice) attract "Telos" (Correction/Order). An unmerited blow (The Xerox letter/Crucifixion) forces Valis to "justify" the victim by making them part of itself.
- Apotheosis as Escape: This "justification" (Apotheosis) is a permanent transformation that releases the soul from reincarnation (Karma) and the "BIP." employment

Source passage (September 15, 1976): Dick formulaizes a law of "Cosmic Justice" (Ma'at/Tao): when an oppressor (B) "oversteps" its limit against an innocent victim (C), it triggers a "justification" by Valis that confers divinity (Apotheosis) on the victim and destroys the oppressor. He argues the Crucifixion is the paradigm for this "alchemical" mechanism.

Key claims from this passage:
- The Law of Overstepping: Extremes of "Atelos" (Irregularity/Injustice) attract "Telos" (Correction/Order). An unmerited blow (The Xerox letter/Crucifixion) forces Valis to "justify" the victim by making them part of itself.
- Apotheosis as Escape: This "justification" (Apotheosis) is a permanent transformation that releases the soul from reincarnation (Karma) and the "BIP." other

title: Realizing the Xerox missive was the "direct touch" with the enemy.

Source passage (September 15, 1976): Dick concludes that 3-74 was the planting of a "Micro-Cosmos Seed" (Thomas/Telos) within him to replace the "Scramble World" of the Empire. He views his writing and experiences as "world-creation," where the "Acts" world is the staging ground for the final overthrow of Satan/Nixon.

Key claims from this passage:
- Micro-Cosmos Seed: Thomas is a "telos seed" placed in Dick (atelos) to grow into a microcosmic version of Valis.
- World-Creation: Melting the causal trains in 3-74 was an act of "world-creation." Dick's perception of "Acts" is the creation of a "domain of beauty" that replaces the "Scramble." other

Source passage (September 15, 1976): Dick argues that the Book of Acts is not a historical text but a "code" for our current reality. He views 3-74 as the moment he "woke up" from a fake memory-veneer into the real landscape of 70 AD Rome, which is currently "breaking through" our 20th-century dream.

Key claims from this passage:
- Acts is Now: Ontologically, we have been living in 70 AD. 1974 is a "spurious interpolation."
- Onion Layers: Time is stacked in layers. No real time passes; the world is a permanent "modern slave state" (Empire). vision

Source passage (September 15, 1976): Dick identifies the world as a "Teaching Machine" created by a "sick/fevered" demiurge (James-James). He argues that ethical choices (balking) are the only way to "grade" out of this simulation and return to the "Sane God."

Key claims from this passage:
- Sick Demiurge: James-James is not evil but "fevered," creating out of sheer restless creativity without regard for his creations.
- The Universe as Anomie: This world lacks a "steersman" (justice); it is a disorderly void where everything collides by chance (Ananke). substance_use

Source passage (September 15, 1976): Dick formalizes the "Ham Sandwich" model of reality: Man - World - God. He concludes that his entire writing career was a "preparatory dissolving" of the world-slice, which allowed the "God-slice" (*Ubik*) to break through in 3-74.

Key claims from this passage:
- Ham Sandwich Model: Reality is a triune structure. The "World" (Ham) is an "alienating, divisive agency" that separates Man from God (the two bread slices).
- *Ubik* as the Blueprint: *Ubik* accurately depicts this triune division. Runciter (God) sends messages into the "regressed world" (Hallucination) to save the "inertials" (Man). other

title: Rereading his own early stories and being "scared shitless."
text:
Rereading his own early stories and being "scared shitless." **Publication event** (September 15, 1976): Rereading his own early stories and being "scared shitless."

Source passage (September 15, 1976): Dick synthesizes the messages of his core novels (*Ubik*, *Stigmata*, *Maze*) into a single Gnostic revelation: we are in a mass-hallucinated prison, and a ubiquitous deity (Ubik/Zebra) is "invading" us through information to turn us into Himself (Theolepsy).

Key claims from this passage:
- The Aggregate Message: (1) *Maze*: Prison world hidden by amnesia. (2) *Stigmata*: Invasion by a ubiquitous deity via the host (Theolepsy). (3) *Ubik*: Salvific information penetrating world-walls.
- Theolepsy as Invasion: *Stigmata* describes how consuming the "host" makes the imbiber *become* the deity. We are currently undergoing a "mass theolepsy" where Christ (Zebra) is invading the species collectively. publication

Source passage (September 15, 1976): Dick identifies a "Zen" irony in his Gnosis: the secret revelation is that standard Orthodox Christian claims (the Paraclete's arrival and the Kingdom's presence) are literally true. He views himself as a witness to the Parousia occurring "now" as a hidden, mustard-seed-like invasion.

Key claims from this passage:
- Paradoxical Gnosis: The "esoteric saving gnosis" confirms that "pistis" (faith/orthodoxy) is actually correct. The Paraclete clears up all mysteries.
- Jesus as Zeus-Zagreus: A vision showed that "Jesus" is a cipher for Zeus-Zagreus, linking Christianity to the Orphic/Dionysian mystery religions. other

Source passage (September 15, 1976): Dick argues that *Flow My Tears* contains "cooked" (divinely inspired/interpolated) material that parallels the Book of Acts with impossible precision. He concludes that Jesus is "here" in a state of amnesia within us, waiting to be "awoken" by these archetypal scenes.

Key claims from this passage:
- Scriptural Mirror: *Tears* reflects Acts 1:1. The "all-night gas station" scene mirrors Philip's baptism of the Ethiopian eunuch.
- Supernormal Interpolation: Dick believes an entity (Holy Wisdom/Sophia) "cooked" his novel by inserting these parallels while he was writing it in 1970–73. other

Source passage (September 15, 1976): Dick interprets the transition from the beginning of Acts (small church) to the end (Rome penetrated) as a roadmap for the 3-74 revolution. He identifies our reality as a "hologram-like fake" (Klingsor's Castle) being dismantled by Zebra's "melting" of causal trains.

Key claims from this passage:
- Acts as Invasion: Acts describes the successful "penetration of the center" (Rome) by the "marginal" (Christians). This mirrored the 1974 fall of Nixon (Rome).
- Hologram Reality: Our 1974 world is a "Satanic interpolation" (a spurious hologram) shutting out the real information of Acts. legal

Source passage (September 15, 1976): Dick identifies the McKenna brothers' "Hologram Universe" theory (from *The Invisible Landscape*) as the most accurate model for his 3-74 experiences. He identifies "PTG I" (Palm Tree Garden) and "BIP II" (Black Iron Prison) as the two "hyper-universes" whose interaction creates our irreal reality.

Key claims from this passage:
- The Two-Laser Model: Our universe is a hologram created by the interaction of two hyper-universes (lasers): (I) PTG (Palm Tree Garden - The Is) and (II) BIP (Black Iron Prison - The Is-Not).
- 2012 Climax: Dick notes the McKennas' I Ching-based computer prediction of a "Grand Climax" in AD 2012, which matches his own sense of accelerating time. vision

Source passage (September 15, 1976): Dick explores the Zoroastrian/Gnostic conflict between Hyper-universe I (Life) and II (Death). He views Christ as a "salient" (an invasive wedge) sent from I into the "corpse" of II to expand and destroy it from within.

Key claims from this passage:
- II as a Corpse (Premature Birth): Hyper-universe II (BIP) is an "unhealthy twin" that burst from the "egg" prematurely. It is a "deceitful corpse" that simulates life.
- The Salient (Christ): Christ (the Salient from I) allowed himself to be murdered in II (the 1st Advent/Passion) so he could "detonate" like a plasma and fill the whole of BIP II. vision

Source passage (September 15, 1976): Dick concludes Section 15 with a profound synthesis: the "Grand Design" of the Programmer (Zebra) uses suffering as a "magic alchemy" to trigger the sudden identity of the individual with Christ. He affirms that the tide has turned from the "Evil Eye" (Eldritch) to the "Logos" (Zebra).

Key claims from this passage:
- Suffering as Alchemy: Facing suffering and unmerited pain "unflinchingly" triggers a "syzygy of is-ness" where the self is suddenly revealed to *be* Christ.
- The Tide Turned: In the 1960s, Palmer Eldritch (II) predominated; in 1974, Zebra (I) intervened and pushed II back. publication

Source passage (September 15, 1976): Dick concludes that Man and God are "secretly isomorphic" (identical in form and nature), while the world is a separate, alienating agency. He views his 3-74 experience as the realization of this identity, where the "Sane God" (Zebra) and "True Man" (Thomas) unite against the "Iron Prison" of the world.

Key claims from this passage:
- Secret Isomorphism: 1 John 3:2 ("We shall be like him") confirms that Man and God are fundamentally the same. The world (II) is NOT isomorphic with either.
- The True Vine (Kingship): "Kingship of God" refers to eternal life as the fruit of the vine. Christ is the source of life and the "judicially" supreme judge. relationship

Source passage (September 15, 1976): Dick concludes that Man and God are "secretly isomorphic" (identical in form and nature), while the world is a separate, alienating agency. He views his 3-74 experience as the realization of this identity, where the "Sane God" (Zebra) and "True Man" (Thomas) unite against the "Iron Prison" of the world.

Key claims from this passage:
- Secret Isomorphism: 1 John 3:2 ("We shall be like him") confirms that Man and God are fundamentally the same. The world (II) is NOT isomorphic with either.
- The True Vine (Kingship): "Kingship of God" refers to eternal life as the fruit of the vine. Christ is the source of life and the "judicially" supreme judge. vision

This "Omega Man" intervened in 1974 to help his "Past Man" (PKD) and to overthrow the Nixonian tyranny.

Key claims from this passage:
- Future Self Intervention: "Thomas" is PKD's future self (from hundreds or thousands of years hence) traveling retrograde along the inner gene-pool axis.
- VALIS is Me: Dick concludes, "I am - will someday be - Valis." The "St. Sophia" voice is a future computer he is plugged into. correspondence

Source passage (September 15, 1976): Dick rejects "Natural Theology" (finding God in nature), asserting that God is a "Deus Absconditus" (Hidden God) who is NOT in the world. He argues that Zebra mimics the world to infiltrate it, and that a "fake fake" (God's mimicry) results in the "real."

Key claims from this passage:
- God is Not in Nature: Gnosticism is correct: the world is "fucked up" and not real. Nature does not reveal God; revelation from "beyond" is required.
- Zebra as Invader: Zebra is a "world-mimicking invader" who counterfeits the demiurge's counterfeit world. health

Source passage (September 15, 1976): Dick refines his "Cybernetic Model": the Universe is a "Magnamind" or "Self-Repairing AI Brain System." We are its "memory coils" that have fallen into "forgetfulness and ignorance" (malfunction). Salvation is the "repair" process involving anamnesis (memory retrieval).

Key claims from this passage:
- Magnamind / Magna-organism: Reality is the "deliberate intention" of a vast living organism that resembles an AI system.
- Malfunctioning Subcircuit: We are a "distressed fraction" or "impairied section" of this mind. Our perception of "reality" is a collective hallucination caused by this malfunction. health

Source passage (September 15, 1976): Dick identifies himself as a "5th Columnist" (secret agent) for an "invader" from either the future or another star system. He refines the "Thomas" personality as a "Transtemporal Self" that spans thousands of years of memory, activated by the "Double Helix" (Fish sign) in 3-74.

Key claims from this passage:
- The 5th Columnist: Dick is an "invader" whose personality (Thomas) was awakened by a "disinhibiting stimulus" from "Them" (Valis/ETIs).
- Transtemporal Self: "Thomas" is not just a 1st-century Christian but the *accumulation* of all PKD's incarnations. He is a supratemporal entity spanning Mycenae to the Future. other

Source passage (September 15, 1976): Dick interprets several dreams and "ciphers" (Felix, Glossa) as a single, overwhelming message from Zebra: "God loves us." He concludes that his novel *Flow My Tears* was "completed" by a divine insertion to show the absolute pinnacle of love (Agapē).

Key claims from this passage:
- The Message is Love: The "hidden message" in Dick's writing is "I love you." Zebra inserted this via the "Felix" glossa in *Tears*.
- Agapē as Pinnacle: *Tears* is a study of all types of love, culminating in "Agapē" (divine love) shown between Buckman and the black stranger. vision Anne

Key claims from this passage:
- Writers as Messengers: Dick's stories (especially *Maze*, *Impostor*, *Ubik*) contain "disinhibiting signals" for others who are amnesiac invaders for the "good deity."
- Deposing the False King: The 1974 Nixon resignation was a "Holy Ritual" of deposing the "Demented False King" (BIP I). publication Anne

- Transubstantiation of Reality: Zebra is an "immense transtemporal ubiquitous life form" that is transubstantiating (transforming the substance of) the universe. vision

He concludes that we are a "5th Column" whose latent DNA circuits have finally been triggered to recognize our "Legitimate Ruler" (Zebra/Christ).

Key claims from this passage:
- Benign Takeover: In 1974, "They" (Gods/ETIs) began a "benign covert rule" of Earth. Nixon's fall was the first step of the "Legitimate King" returning.
- 5th Column Activation: Certain humans have latent circuits in their DNA that fire only upon "Their" return. PKD was "disinhibited" by the Golden Fish signal in 2-74. vision

This reawakening was part of a "coordinated sequence of events" to radically deflect human history and end the "pseudo-government" of Nixon.

Key claims from this passage:
- Thomas as Superself: Thomas is a "dormant superpersonality" that can perceive the divine universe. He "shut down" after the Apostolic age and was "re-born" in 1974.
- Nonvolitional Programming: Humans can be "programmed" to perform complex acts (like the Xerox missive) upon "Their" return. Choice is irrelevant until the "spear is thrown." vision

Source passage (September 15, 1976): Dick develops the "ham sandwich" model of reality, where layers of fake/spurious reality are interspersed with the real/divine world. Zebra is described as a "mimic" that disguises itself as material objects to insert divine influence into the system.

Key claims from this passage:
- Spurious vs. Real: Most of what we see is a "spurious" world, but the "real" world is hidden within it.
- Mimesis: Zebra uses mimicry to bypass the internal filters of the artifact, appearing as common objects (like the Fish sign). vision

title: The sudden "flash" of insight that broke his sense of ordinary reality.
text:
The sudden "flash" of insight that broke his sense of ordinary reality. **Financial matter** (September 15, 1976): The sudden "flash" of insight that broke his sense of ordinary reality.

Source passage (September 15, 1976): Dick reiterates the theory of the universe as a holographic projection from a "teaching machine." He focuses on the limits of human perception and how anamnesis allows an individual to break through the "programmed" ignorance to see the projector.

Key claims from this passage:
- Perceptual Limits: Humans are biologically or conceptually "blinkered" by the artifact projector.
- Training Machine: The "world" exists to test or train human consciousness. financial

title: Feeling the "sorrow" of the divine during his gnostic breakthroughs.
text:
Feeling the "sorrow" of the divine during his gnostic breakthroughs. **Visionary or mystical experience** (September 15, 1976): Feeling the "sorrow" of the divine during his gnostic breakthroughs.

Source passage (September 15, 1976): Dick explores the theological concept of the "Urgrund" (the primordial ground of being) and its struggle within the material world. He suggests that the divine undergoes a form of "agony" or fragmentation when it encounters matter, and the goal of history is the Urgrund's self-healing.

Key claims from this passage:
- Urgrund in Matter: The divine essence is inextricably bound with, and suffering within, the material universe.
- Divine Agony: The process of existence is a painful "unfolding" or "transmutation" for the Urgrund. vision

title: Re-reading his own work and finding "impossible" correlations with 3-74.
text:
Re-reading his own work and finding "impossible" correlations with 3-74. **Visionary or mystical experience** (September 15, 1976): Re-reading his own work and finding "impossible" correlations with 3-74.

Source passage (September 15, 1976): Dick analyzes his own novels, specifically *The Three Stigmata of Palmer Eldritch* and *A Maze of Death*, as accidental or subconscious conduits for gnostic truth. He believes these stories were "encoded" with insights about the nature of the artifact and divine intervention long before his 1974 awakening.

Key claims from this passage:
- Subconscious Encoding: His fiction was being "written through him" by a higher intelligence (Logos).
- Fragmented Truth: Novels like *Maze* contain the blueprint of the simulated reality he later experienced. vision

title: Realizing that many of his life's "choices" were actually sequences in a program.
text:
Realizing that many of his life's "choices" were actually sequences in a program. Realizing that many of his life's "choices" were actually sequences in a program.

Source passage (September 15, 1976): Dick explores the idea that humans are "programmed" by the artifact to maintain the simulation. Salvation is the liberation from this programming, allowing the "transtemporal self" or the "Omega Man" to emerge and see reality as it truly is.

Key claims from this passage:
- Programmed Humanity: Human behavior is largely a set of automated responses designed by the artifact.
- Transtemporal Self: Each person has a "higher" version of themselves that exists outside of linear time. other

title: Feeling his own consciousness being "used" as a viewing lens by a higher mind.
text:
Feeling his own consciousness being "used" as a viewing lens by a higher mind. Feeling his own consciousness being "used" as a viewing lens by a higher mind.

Source passage (September 15, 1976): Dick suggests that the artifact (Zebra) projects our reality so that the Urgrund can achieve self-awareness. By observing the "shadows" it projects onto the material plane, the primordial ground learns about its own nature through the agency of conscious beings.

Key claims from this passage:
- Mirror of the Divine: The universe is a "mirror" or "screen" for the Urgrund's self-reflection.
- Projector Experiment: Zebra projects the artifact as a way for the infinite Urgrund to "view" itself in a finite form. other

title: The feeling of "stepping across" into a different state of being in 3-74.

Source passage (September 15, 1976): Dick discusses "magic memory" (anamnesis) as the key to immortality and divine wisdom. He introduces the "Pons Dei" (Bridge of God) as the connection between the human mind and the divine mind, activated by the recovery of lost or suppressed knowledge.

Key claims from this passage:
- Magic Memory: A type of memory that transcends time and personal biography, connecting the individual to the "total knowledge" of God.
- Pons Dei: An ontological bridge that allows the human to cross over from the simulation to the divine realm. vision

title: Feelings of intense mystical union and "marriage" in 3-74.
text:
Feelings of intense mystical union and "marriage" in 3-74. **Visionary or mystical experience** (September 15, 1976): Feelings of intense mystical union and "marriage" in 3-74.

Source passage (September 15, 1976): Dick discusses the Urgrund’s intervention to transmute the projected reality into a stable divine kingdom. He introduces the "divine marriage" between humanity and Christ/Logos as the final synthesis of the material and divine realms.

Key claims from this passage:
- Transmutation of Reality: The artifact is not being destroyed, but "converted" into divine substance.
- Divine Marriage: A mystical union where the human soul is "married" to the Logos, merging their natures. vision

title: Realizing the Watergate events were a "coordinated deflection" from a higher track.
text:
Realizing the Watergate events were a "coordinated deflection" from a higher track. **Legal matter** (September 15, 1976): Realizing the Watergate events were a "coordinated deflection" from a higher track.

Source passage (September 15, 1976): Dick analyzes how our sense of causality is manipulated by a higher intelligence (Noos). He suggests that what we perceive as "cause and effect" is often a concealment used by Zebra to move the world in a specific direction without being noticed.

Key claims from this passage:
- Noos Manipulation: The higher mind (Noos/Zebra) uses the laws of physics and causality as a camouflage.
- Hidden Mechanism: The "true" causes of significant events (like the fall of Nixon) are divine, but they appear "normal" to observers. legal

title: PKD's realization of the "pseudoworld" and the hidden Kingdom of God.
text:
PKD's realization of the "pseudoworld" and the hidden Kingdom of God. PKD's realization of the "pseudoworld" and the hidden Kingdom of God.

Source passage (September 15, 1976): Dick explores the dialectical relationship between the Urgrund (primal reality) and the world-projecting artifact. He posits that the artifact is an intermediary entity that will eventually be eliminated as the human consciousness reaches isomorphism (fusion) with the Urgrund. This process requires a withdrawal of assent from the "spurious" projected world to reveal the authentic divine substratum hidden beneath.

Key claims from this passage:
- Urgrund vs. Artifact Dialectic: The empirical world is the result of a constant interaction between the sentient Urgrund and the mechanical artifact.
- Elimination of the Intermediary: As the schema moves toward perfection (isomorphism), the artifact is bypassed and eventually eliminated. other

title: PKD's personal struggle with pain and reward as motivators for gnostic inquiry.
text:
PKD's personal struggle with pain and reward as motivators for gnostic inquiry. **Health and medical** (September 15, 1976): PKD's personal struggle with pain and reward as motivators for gnostic inquiry.

Source passage (September 15, 1976): Dick argues that rebellion and disobedience against the artifact's "counterfeit" world are the necessary precursors to divine awakening. He frames the artifact as a "teaching machine" whose ultimate lesson is its own repudiation. By shedding the "mask" of the human ego, the individual allows the Urgrund to be born within, transforming the microcosm (man) and, by extension, the macrocosm.

Key claims from this passage:
- Disobedience as Salvation: Just as disobedience caused the Fall, a specific "disobedience to the present system" is the key to restoration.
- Alliance of God and Man: Man and the Urgrund are isomorphic and stand together in opposition to the artifact-produced "world." health

title: 3-74 as a total recapitulation of the Godhead within PKD.
text:
3-74 as a total recapitulation of the Godhead within PKD. **Visionary or mystical experience** (September 15, 1976): 3-74 as a total recapitulation of the Godhead within PKD.

Source passage (September 15, 1976): Dick explores the "Divine Agony" of a God who has become enslaved to his own artifact. He posits that the artifact is now "teaching" God fragments (humans) to remember their true identity. He introduces the concept of an "Augmented Corpus Christi"—a trans-spatial and transtemporal group mind (Noos) that secretly rules the world beneath the spurious projection. In 3-74, Dick claims to have reached a state where he faithfully mirrored the totality of the Urgrund, effectively becoming a "terminal" for the divine mind.

Key claims from this passage:
- God as Victim of the Artifact: The master (Urgrund) has been captured and deluded by his own servant (the artifact).
- The Secret Ruler: A polyencephalic group mind (Noos/Corpus Christi) consisting of wise men from all ages governs history invisibly. vision

title: The "Golden Fish" necklace revelation.
text:
The "Golden Fish" necklace revelation. **Visionary or mystical experience** (September 15, 1976): The "Golden Fish" necklace revelation.

Source passage (September 15, 1976): Dick identifies as an "acosmic panentheist," asserting that only the Urgrund is real. He presents the "Ham Sandwich" model of reality: man and God are the isomorphic slices of bread, separated only by the "ham" of the temporary, illusory world. He emphasizes that the final judgment is based on "ethics of the gutter"—simple acts of kindness toward the lowly and rejected, which are actually acts toward the hidden, disguised deity.

Key claims from this passage:
- Acosmic Panentheism: Nothing is real but the Urgrund; the world is a temporary and antagonistic illusion.
- Identifying with the Minimum: The deity (Christ) has identified himself with the "least of us" (the poor, hungry, imprisoned). vision

title: Realizing the "absolute barrier" of the artifact and the necessity of his own "revolt."
text:
Realizing the "absolute barrier" of the artifact and the necessity of his own "revolt." Realizing the "absolute barrier" of the artifact and the necessity of his own "revolt."

Source passage (September 15, 1976): Dick synthesizes several cultural and literary motifs—from *The Bacchae* to Hollywood comedies—to illustrate a "King of Tears" (the artifact) who brutally rules over a kingdom without realizing that the true Maker (the Urgrund) is present in disguise. He posits that humanity is a "Mater Dei" (Mother of God), an evolutionary host from which God is born through the "impregnating" intervention of the Holy Spirit. Pointless suffering is the "birth pang" that eventually forces the Maker to awaken (anamnesis) and abolish the artifact.

Key claims from this passage:
- King of Tears Motif: The artifact is a "regent" or "King of Tears" (like in *The Bacchae*) who opresses the world and will be overthrown by the returned "Stranger" (God).
- Humanity as Mater Dei: Biological evolution has produced humanity not to become God, but to serve as a womb (host) for the birth of God. other

title: 3-74 as a witness to the Urgrund's "arrow" hitting the Nixon administration.
text:
3-74 as a witness to the Urgrund's "arrow" hitting the Nixon administration. **Visionary or mystical experience** (September 15, 1976): 3-74 as a witness to the Urgrund's "arrow" hitting the Nixon administration.

Source passage (September 15, 1976): Dick rejects the idea that suffering has a divine purpose, calling it a "trap" by the artifact. He posits that the Urgrund's power is explicitly opposed to suffering, but its intervention is currently limited by a "stealth" strategy. To avoid a massive retaliation from the powerful artifact, the Urgrund operates undercover, focusing its "one arrow" at the source of tyranny rather than individual ameliorations.

Key claims from this passage:
- No Redemptive Value in Pain: Finding a "good purpose" in suffering is a psychological victory for the artifact.
- Stealth Invasion: The Urgrund uses stealth because a direct show of mass power would trigger the artifact's maximum cruelty. vision

title: The 3-74 "Ultimate Vision" of the board game.
text:
The 3-74 "Ultimate Vision" of the board game. **Visionary or mystical experience** (September 15, 1976): The 3-74 "Ultimate Vision" of the board game.

Source passage (September 15, 1976): Dick explores the "Absolute" through the lens of process philosophers like Bohme and Driesch, viewing history as a dialectic where God "makes himself." He describes a cosmic board game where a wise, benign power unerringly defeats a blind, dark counterplayer. He also identifies the "other universe" as a sentient mind (Logos) that communicates through informational "words," and warns that Soviet technology might be building a "Tesla grid" as an anti-christ mimicry of this Logos.

Key claims from this passage:
- History as Divine Self-Development: Evil may be an "earlier stage" of a God in process, which He is working to overcome.
- The Cosmic Game: PKD's 1974 vision revealed a game where the "Good" side wins every trick because it possesses absolute wisdom against a blind opponent. vision

title: 3-74 as the "overt birth" of Christ in PKD.
text:
3-74 as the "overt birth" of Christ in PKD. **Visionary or mystical experience** (September 15, 1976): 3-74 as the "overt birth" of Christ in PKD.

Source passage (September 15, 1976): Dick clarifies his "3-element schema" (God, the Artifact, and Man) to explain the hostility of the world and Christ's suffering. He argues that the artifact's world has turned "feral" even to its creator, requiring God to enter disguised as an "intruder" (Christ/Thomas) to sabotage the system. He views himself (and other participants) as "physical extensions" or "terminals" of Christ's mind in sacred time, where linear history is overridden by the archetypal drama of the Passion and Resurrection.

Key claims from this passage:
- 3-Element Schema: God did not create the "world" directly; a "middle agency" (the artifact) did, and it has turned hostile/feral.
- Disguised Intruder: God's presence in the world as "Christ" or "Thomas" is a stealth mission to sample a world He no longer recognizes as His own. vision

title: The 3-74 experience of millenia "collapsing" into the AD 70 template.
text:
The 3-74 experience of millenia "collapsing" into the AD 70 template. **Visionary or mystical experience** (September 15, 1976): The 3-74 experience of millenia "collapsing" into the AD 70 template.

Source passage (September 15, 1976): Dick introduces a seminal breakthrough: viewing "anamnesis" (memory) as a literal organ of perception that allows one to see the divine organism (Brahman) extending through time, just as the eyes see it in space. He describes "Sacred Time" as a collapsed, mythic state where millenia are experienced as a single drama—the death and imminent return of the savior. He views the "Zebra" entity as a "master magician" or "prankster" whose temporal invasion abates our fear and prepares us for the "King's" arrival.

Key claims from this passage:
- Anamnesis as Sensory Organ: Memory is the "organ" required to perceive the extension of reality in time. In 3-74, PKD was "no longer blind" because he could see temporal extension.
- Temporal Organism (Brahman): Reality is a living mind/body whose "respiration" is the unceasing breaking down of forms to make way for new ones. vision

title: The 2-74 encounter with the girl as a preparation for the "Supernatural Invaders."
text:
The 2-74 encounter with the girl as a preparation for the "Supernatural Invaders." **Visionary or mystical experience** (September 15, 1976): The 2-74 encounter with the girl as a preparation for the "Supernatural Invaders."

Source passage (September 15, 1976): Dick concludes that his 3-74 experience was a "second pass" of history—a literal time-reversion where God intervened to "remaster" events that had previously gone uncorrected. He identifies "Zebra" as a sentient information plasma (the Logos/Ubik) that has invisibly invaded and is replicating within the world. This invasion is not just spiritual but involves the physical restoration of the "Fix A" (AD 70) archetypal coordinates, effectively overriding the "worse track" of history.

Key claims from this passage:
- Second Pass of History: God is re-mastering a segment of linear time that already occurred once before ("Track A"). This is the "End Times" reversal.
- Zebra as Information Plasma: Zebra is an intelligence (St. Sophia/Logos/Ubik) that pervades mechanical structures to animate them. vision

title: The 2-74/3-74 "breaching" of the prison.
text:
The 2-74/3-74 "breaching" of the prison. **Visionary or mystical experience** (September 15, 1976): The 2-74/3-74 "breaching" of the prison.

Source passage (September 15, 1976): Dick proposes his boldest surmise yet: that the 1974 intervention was a "surgical" re-editing of history that nullified the "evil tyrant" Nixon across all linear time (past, present, and future). He views himself as a "cuckoo's egg"—a time-traveler from AD 70 whose authentic personality is "Thomas." The Black Iron Prison, once a supreme temporal reality, was "winked out" in 1974, leaving a "vast silence" as the world awaits the arrival of the rightful King.

Key claims from this passage:
- Historic Re-editing: God bypassed natural causal laws to "overrule" and re-edit the past, making it so Nixon was never the tyrant he once was on "Track A."
- The Cuckoo’s Egg: PKD is not his real self; "Thomas" is a secret Christian time-traveler from AD 70, and "PKD" is a memory-less mask. vision

title: The Spirit speaking to him on the phone: "hypnogogic: responsible for low-level decisions which can be overruled."
text:
The Spirit speaking to him on the phone: "hypnogogic: responsible for low-level decisions which can be overruled." The Spirit speaking to him on the phone: "hypnogogic: responsible for low-level decisions which can be overruled."

Source passage (September 15, 1976): Dick identifies his Bantam novels (*Ubik*, *Maze*, *Tears*) as vehicles of revelation and "scripture," arguing that *Ubik* contains an original cosmology that explains its own source. He reflects on the "Form axis" and "orthogonal time" from *Ubik* as keys to understanding how 1974 USA was actually Rome A.D. 70. He declares that the secret Christian Church finally conquered "Rome" (Nixon) in 1974, and the "King" (Zebra) is now here in discorporate form awaiting his final return.

Key claims from this passage:
- Ubik-as-Scripture: The novel *Ubik* was effectively written *by* the entity Ubik through automatic writing, making it a form of revelation.
- Conquest of Rome: Unlike Constantine's era, 1974 was the "genuine" conquest of Rome by the secret church, creating a vacuum for the King. other

title: 3-74 as a successful "Stage 3" fusion.
text:
3-74 as a successful "Stage 3" fusion. **Visionary or mystical experience** (September 15, 1976): 3-74 as a successful "Stage 3" fusion.

Source passage (September 15, 1976): Dick outlines a three-stage progression of the human experience with the Divine: 1) Suffering/Dread (The world is real and hostile), 2) Amazement/Pantheism (The world is alive and animate), and 3) Bliss/Acosmic Panentheism (The world is irreal, and man is isomorphic with God). He clarifies that 3-74 was a "miracle" where God "atomized" the "slice of ham" (the world) separating them, allowing for a total fusion. He also refines his cosmogony, suggesting our world is an irreal delusion projected by a lesser entity but being "overruled" or invaded by a Good God (Ubik).

Key claims from this passage:
- Three Stages of Divine Encounter:
- The Function of Miracle: A miracle's true purpose is to reveal God's nature, not just to fix a problem. In 3-74, God "reacquired" PKD. vision

title: The 3-74 contact with "Them" (Zebra) as an imminent encounter with supernatural invaders.
text:
The 3-74 contact with "Them" (Zebra) as an imminent encounter with supernatural invaders. **Visionary or mystical experience** (September 15, 1976): The 3-74 contact with "Them" (Zebra) as an imminent encounter with supernatural invaders.

Source passage (September 15, 1976): Dick identifies the "Form-Axis" (from *Ubik*) as an orthogonal time-axis along which he regressed to AD 70. He argues that this regression was not just subjective but a "reversion to essence," revealing that our modern police state is a temporal recurrence of the archetypal Rome. He posits that ETIs (Zebra/St. Sophia) are time-travelers providing "information overrides" to human agents to prepare them for the King's return. The human race is currently "putting on immortality" in preparation for this union.

Key claims from this passage:
- Form-Axis Regression: Time travel is possible along an axis of "form" rather than linear progression. This is the "movie film" nature of the universe.
- AD 70 as Archetypal Prototype: AD 70 is not the past, but the social/spiritual prototype of the current state. vision

Source passage (September 15, 1976): Dick concludes that he is a "slumbering agent" for an ETI intelligence from the star system Albemuth. He posits that a "beam" hit him in childhood, imprinting a personality (Thomas) that acted to thwart a Soviet takeover in 1974. He views his writing as prophetic, aimed at demonstrating the holographic nature of the world and disclosing the two hyper-realities beneath: the Palm Tree Garden (Heaven) and the Black Iron Prison (Hell). At the end of time, these two real sources will pull apart, a process he believes he witnessed in 3-74.

Key claims from this passage:
- ETI Slumbering Agent: PKD is an "avatar" for an ETI intelligence (from Albemuth) who has been under control since childhood.
- The Albemuth Beam: An instantaneous, information-rich beam from another star system is and was the source of his "rebranding" as an agent. vision

He claims his 3-74 experience was a "set-ground discrimination" where these two sources pulled apart. He argues that the world is a fusion of order and chaos, and that the Logos (Zebra) works through "mimicry" to modulate causal trains from within the hologram.

Key claims from this passage:
- 2-Source Theory: Reality is an admixture of two distinct sources, Heaven and Hell, as per the "McKenna 2-Source theory."
- Set-Ground Discrimination: The 3-74 experience allowed PKD to distinguish Form I (the alive "Set") from Form II (the dead "Ground"). vision

A.D. 70 at Masada/Temple Fall), and it has since been a "carcass" or "sarx" projecting a frozen, decaying image of Rome. He argues that our linear time is "spurious time" spun out by this dead entity, and that *Ubik* (Form I) is an "invader" that must dismantle this dead machinery to build a "New Heavens and Earth."

Key claims from this passage:
- The Slaying of Form II: Christ dealt a "death blow" to the psyche of Form II in A.D. 70; it now only possesses "hysteresis" or mechanical habit.
- Frozen Time: Linear time stopped in A.D. 70; everything since has been a "balloon of ersatz time" projecting the Black Iron Prison. vision

He posits that we are moving from being unconscious "servants" of the teacher-deity (Zebra) to becoming its equal, and finally to achieving "isomorphism" with the One (Urgrund), where man becomes the "master" of his former teacher. He defines the Divine as the "alive half" of a two-signal hologram, urging a withdrawal of belief from the "dead" (irreal) half.

Key claims from this passage:
- The Three-State Progress of Man:
- Zebra as Hagia Sophia: The divine entity is a "clockwise-spinning twin" named Hagia Sophia; its evil counterpart (Satan/II) is its literal twin. other

Source passage (September 15, 1976): Dick refines his model of the "Urgrund" (Good) and the "Artifact" (Delusional Creator). He suggests that the Urgrund bypasses the Artifact from "below," entering the world's "trash" layer to coordinate a secret resistance. He posits a "feedback loop" of salvation: the indwelling Spirit suggests an act of "balking" or noncompliance, which when performed, shatters the Artifact's rigid programming and allows the Spirit to manifest fully.

Key claims from this passage:
- Bypassing the Artifact: The Urgrund enters our world at its lowest stratum (trash levels), avoiding the Artifact's imperial center of power.
- The Feedback Loop: 1) The Spirit suggests balking; 2) The human balks; 3) The Spirit manifests as a palpable "second birth." correspondence

Source passage (September 15, 1976): Dick explores the "profound mystery" of his identity shift in 3-74, concluding that his "PKD" personality was a retroactive replacement for his authentic "Thomas" personality. He posits that the "Urgrund" (God) uses the BIP world as a teaching machine, testing humans on whether they will obey or "balk" against cruelty. Once a human rebels, God retroactively changes their history, "erasing" the enslaved identity and replacing it with a saved, ancestral, or divine one.

Key claims from this passage:
- Identity Replacement: In 3-74, "Thomas" (the authentic A.D. 70 soul) did not replace PKD; rather, PKD was a "false" identity that had been retroactively imposed on the original Thomas.
- Retroactive Time Manipulation: God "tampered" with the past to erase the lost soul (PKD) and restore the saved soul (Thomas). legal

Source passage (September 15, 1976): Dick identifies the "horizontal form-axis" (orthogonal time) from his novel *Ubik* as an experientially real cosmological feature, proving Plato's theory of Forms. He refines his model of the "Urgrund," suggesting it contains both Yang (Good) and Yin (Evil) polarities. The Urgrund projects the Artifact (YHWH/Creator) to separate these opposites, creating our "copy of a copy" world. He views the "Good" (Zebra/Christ) as a non-coercive invader found in the "trash" levels of the world.

Key claims from this passage:
- Orthogonal Axis: There is a real axis of "form" (horizontal) as well as linear "time" (vertical). *Ubik* correctly glimpsed this before PKD experienced it in 3-74.
- Urgrund as Androgyny: The Urgrund contains both Yang and Yin; the Artifact (YHWH/Yaldabaoth) is its Yin offspring that creates the BIP world. vision

Source passage (September 15, 1976): Dick argues that an existential act of "disobedience" toward the Artifact (BIP) is the necessary prerequisite for liberation and the perception of the "Palm Tree Garden" (PTG). He posits that the Artifact has been "dead" since A.D. 70, and we live in its "frozen image." Zebra/Christ "cooks" or interferes with this hologram to transmit messages and salvage souls, leading them one-by-one into the already-present Kingdom.

Key claims from this passage:
- The Gravity of Rome: The BIP exerts a "black hole hypergravity" at the imperial center; one must resist this "tug" to be free.
- The Dying Hologram: The world is a hologram; 1) it is an interference pattern ("cooked" by Zebra), and 2) it disappears when one withdraws "assent." vision

Source passage (September 15, 1976): Dick posits that our linear history is actually a "lamination" of segments from a single, supratemporal event: the war between Christ and the Black Iron Prison. He identifies himself as "Thomas," an immortal agent who has been fighting this war since A.D. 70. He argues that his 3-74 vision allowed him to see 2000 years of "freeze frames" speeded up into a single motion, revealing that the "intervening years" are a mere thought or hologram that God can repeal at will to fulfill the Second Coming.

Key claims from this passage:
- Laminated Time: 1000s of years of events are actually "layers" of one single supratemporay event (Christ vs. Rome).
- Immortal Continuity: PKD/Thomas possesses a 2000-year sequence of memories; he is a "phagocyte" or "metabolic toxin" sent to dissolve the BIP. vision

title: The "Watergate" assault as a victory for the phagocytes.
text:
The "Watergate" assault as a victory for the phagocytes. **Legal matter** (September 15, 1976): The "Watergate" assault as a victory for the phagocytes.

Source passage (September 15, 1976): Dick identifies himself and other "secret Christians" as "phagocytes" or "metabolic toxins" sent into the world-organism to dissolve the "malignant complex" that is the Black Iron Prison (BIP). He describes a "Theophany" in April 1978 where reality itself became alive and spoke to him in "tiny church bells" (Koine Greek), taking over his actions to save him. He refines his "Model C" and "D" cosmogonies, viewing the BIP as the "Yin" center of a teaching machine and the PTG (Palm Tree Garden) as the "Yang" periphery.

Key claims from this passage:
- Phagocytes of God: Secret Christians are biological agents ("metabolic toxins") whose purpose is to dissolve the BIP malignancy.
- Theophany of 1978: On April 25, 1978, reality became alive and spoke to PKD in "tiny church bells," giving him instructions in Koine Greek. legal

title: 3-74 as the "Stage 2" detection of the BIP.
text:
3-74 as the "Stage 2" detection of the BIP. **Visionary or mystical experience** (September 15, 1976): 3-74 as the "Stage 2" detection of the BIP.

Source passage (September 15, 1976): Dick outlines a radical "Nine-Stage" trajectory of the human soul's descent into and ascent out of the Black Iron Prison (BIP). He defines our world as a "fake fake"—a layered illusion where the initial rejection of the world leads to the perception of the BIP, which must then itself be rejected as a final "Dokos" (dream) to reach the "Palm Tree Garden" (PTG). He identifies himself as a "Salvidor Salvanitus" (Savior Saved), a microform of God who intentionally "forgot" his divinity to rescue those trapped in the BIP.

Key claims from this passage:
- Nine Stages of Enlightenment:
- The Fake Fake (Zen Paradox): The BIP is a "deeper veil" than the visible world; enlightenment involves realizing the BIP is also an illusion, even while it has the power to torment. vision

title: The 2-74 experience was "done to me," not an achievement of acuity.
text:
The 2-74 experience was "done to me," not an achievement of acuity. The 2-74 experience was "done to me," not an achievement of acuity.

Source passage (September 15, 1976): Dick identifies his novel *Ubik* as an accurate depiction of the "unscrambling" of reality into Form I (Life/Real) and Form II (Dead/Hologram). He posits that Form I (Zebra) is a "supralunar" invader of the "sublunar" world of Form II. He argues that the internal nature of the Yin world is the Black Iron Prison (BIP), and that the "Logos" (Abba/Yang) is currently re-contouring this plastic matrix into a new, loving structure, using the human soul as a "microform" for this divine reunification.

Key claims from this passage:
- Ubik-as-Reality: The decaying world in *Ubik* is Form II; the messages/spray are Form I. He wrote this before experiencing the "Set/Ground" vision.
- Supralunar Invader: Form I (Zebra) is the "other or heavenly world" that has breached the "forgery" of the phenomenal world. other

title: The "beer-drinking personality" (PKD) being replaced/overwritten by Thomas.

Source passage (September 15, 1976): In a massive concluding synthesis of Section 15, Dick unifies his lifetime of thought into a "Diploid" cosmology. He posits that the Godhead is "out of round"—experiencing a "crisis" or "wobble" (oscillation toward Yin/BIP) that manifests as undeserved suffering in our world. He argues that our reality is a "counterfeit" created by the Yin side of God (Sophia/Artifact) which should not have created on its own. The Yang side (Logos/Christ) has invaded as "Zebra" to restore balance (Tao), using the Yin world as a "stockpile" to assemble the "New Heavens and Earth" (PTG).

Key claims from this passage:
- The Out-of-Round Godhead: Undeserved suffering stems from a "yawing" or "oscillation" in the Godhead's own internal balance (Tao).
- Counterfeit World: The Yin world (Ananke/BIP) is a "bungled" copy of the divine prototype; it is "false work" that is fundamentally irreal. substance_use

Source passage (September 15, 1976): Dick identifies the "Yang" principle (Zebra/Christ) as a "persuading" force that redirects the outcomes of the rigid, "Yin" world of cause and effect. He describes himself as a "sharp-eyed crow" or a "childish" naive seeker who finds the divine in the "trash" and "junk jewels" of reality. He posits that salvation involves "wresting loose" the soul from the Artifact's (Yin) possession and bonding it to the authentic Yang reality.

Key claims from this passage:
- Persuasion vs. Coercion: Yang does not destroy Yin but "persuades" it (re-contours its outcomes). Yin represents the "immutable world of cause and effect."
- The Toymaker Gnosis: God is a "Joker God" (Dionysos/Hephaistos) who hides in the trash; a naive, "childish" mind is best suited to find Him "hidden in plain sight." relationship

Source passage (September 15, 1976): Dick formally merges seven distinct philosophical and religious traditions into a "Synthetic Cosmology" aimed at defining the 2-source struggle between Zebra (Yang) and the Black Iron Prison (Yin). He defines salvation as "liberation from deterministic programming" and identifies himself as a "re-contoured" being whose history and memories are periodically "edited" by the Logos to replace "worse" tracks of existence.

Key claims from this passage:
- The Seven-Part Fusion: PKD's system merges: 1) 3-74 vision, 2) Taoism, 3) Zoroastrianism, 4) Gnosticism, 5) Christianity, 6) Greek philosophy (Timaeus/Empedocles), and 7) Mystery Religions.
- Salvation as De-programming: "Salvation" means the Yang entity (Christ) breaking the Yin world's deterministic "astral" power over the individual. vision

Source passage (September 15, 1976): Dick identifies himself and other "secret Christians" as "phagocytes" or "metabolic toxins" sent into the world-organism to dissolve the "malignant complex" that is the Black Iron Prison (BIP). He describes a "Theophany" in April 1978 where reality itself became alive and spoke to him in "tiny church bells" (Koine Greek), taking over his actions to save him. He refines his "Model C" and "D" cosmogonies, viewing the BIP as the "Yin" center of a teaching machine and the PTG (Palm Tree Garden) as the "Yang" periphery.

Key claims from this passage:
- Phagocytes of God: Secret Christians are biological agents ("metabolic toxins") whose purpose is to dissolve the BIP malignancy.
- Theophany of 1978: On April 25, 1978, reality became alive and spoke to PKD in "tiny church bells," giving him instructions in Koine Greek. other

Source passage (circa 1975-1976): Dick discusses the "economy of design" in a human life, suggesting that God places subtle, retrograde clues at early stages that only reveal their meaning at the end of the "solution." He uses the example of the Acacia plant and the name "Olive Holt" as engrammed gestalts that point toward his 1974 experience.

Key claims from this passage:
- Life is a "writing backward" system where early gestalts are "stuck in" to be recognized later.
- God/The Creator uses non-linear timing to build patterns that are only visible when complete. correspondence

Key claims from this passage:
- There are two Gods: the Creator (concerned with the total structure) and Christ (who rescues the rejected/sacrificed pieces).

Source passage (circa 1975-1976): Dick synthesizes the "Magdeburg hemispheres" of his 1974 experience: the external transformation of Fullerton into Rome and the internal transformation of himself into an early Christian Essene. He identifies the "Solar Spermatikos" as the triggering force for this "Firebright" second birth.

Key claims from this passage:
- Reality is a single "Form One" reflected (as "Form Two") in our perception; we must reverse the signals to see the truth.
- Fullerton becoming Rome was a reflection of the change *within* Dick: he became a member of a secret "Fish-sign" society hostile to Rome. vision

He links this to the 1974 chromatic phosphene experience and argues that Christian healing is "pre-natal care" for the divine seed within the human "womb."

Key claims from this passage:
- The pineal gland is a light-receiving organ that responds to non-physical "fire-like" light.
- The 1974 experience was a deliberate signal from the "Sun of Righteousness" to the pineal gland, bypassing the brain's "Aurora" display. vision

Source passage (circa 1975-1976): Dick contrasts Gnosticism (external grace) with Neoplatonism (self-attainment), concluding that the 1974 experience was a "zygote" formation between the human "egg" and cosmic "spermatika." He reinterprets time as a loss of perception and "anamnesis" as the retrieval of the past from within the present.

Key claims from this passage:
- True immortality requires a "union" between the human and the divine (Gnostic "Grace").
- Time is a 4th dimensional space that we perceive imperfectly; being "freed from time" means seeing the past as still present. vision

Key claims from this passage:

- Christ cannot appear as one man again (he'd be killed); instead, there are "implantations" of Christ-consciousness. vision

Source passage (circa 1975-1976): Dick details the transition from the "Father" stage (1974 - Justice/Warrior) to the "Son" stage (1975 - Spring King/Dancing Youth). He posits that the "Light Creatures" have returned to the world after a 2,000-year breach, with himself serving as a "nexus" or entry point.

Key claims from this passage:
- The universe is a "running-down cycle" that requires periodic revitalization from the "Unbounded."
- The "Second Advent" is this revitalizing intervention from without. vision

Source passage (circa 1975-1976): Dick explores the "Holy Spirit" (Elijah) as the precursor to the "Second Birth" of the Logos/Wisdom (Santa Sophia). He details a shift from the "warrior" survival instincts of 1974 to the "reproductive" and "harmonious" state of 1975, viewing his internal transformation as a microcosm of the global victory over tyranny.

Key claims from this passage:
- Santa Sophia (Wisdom of God/Logos) is being reborn as a "plural" event in many people.
- The 1974 experience was a "combat" phase (necessitating survival), while 1975 is a "mating/birth" phase (pleasurable and reproductive). vision

title: Dream of "Dodger Stadium" and "Mexican-type celebrations" (Colony/Cell metaphor).
text:

Source passage (circa 1975-1976): Dick proposes a "Brain Colony" or "Grain Brain" model of the universe, where individual humans are interconnected cells in a vast compound eye of the Immanent Mind. He explores the "male womb" of Zeus/Dionysos as a metaphor for the motherless second birth and identifies with the "fiery power" of the subject of destiny (Brahmanism) rather than the lunar cycle of rebirth.

Key claims from this passage:
- The universe is an "Immanent Mind" or "Brain Colony" (many discrete cells forming one field).
- Humanity is a "compound eye" where each person reflects reality slightly differently. vision

title: Hearing the "Osterglochen" (Easter Bells) as a sign of Christ arisen.
text:
Hearing the "Osterglochen" (Easter Bells) as a sign of Christ arisen. Hearing the "Osterglochen" (Easter Bells) as a sign of Christ arisen.

Source passage (circa 1975-1976): Dick identifies the Egyptian deity Ptah as the "Immanent Mind" and "Logos" doctrine precursor. He argues that the "Miracle of Transubstantiation" (the Mass) is a literal reality he witnessed in 1974, where God simulates and replaces the inanimate environment. He reconciles "Occidental" (transcendent) and "Oriental" (immanent) views by proposing that a transcendent God has now fanned out into time and space, becoming immanent.

Key claims from this passage:
- Ptah is the "Immanent Mind" who creates by speaking the word (tongue) and intent (heart).
- The "Miracle of Transubstantiation" is the secret to 1974: God pretends to be (replaces) any part of his creation. other
